content.full_text:
DAVID R. CHAPMAN
CITY ATTORNEY

JEFFREY R. EPP
ASSISTANT CITY ATTORNEY

MARK A. WAGGONER
ASSISTANT CITY ATTORNEY/LITIGATION

MARTIN A. GROVER
SENIOR DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY

CAROLYN A. ARNOLD
DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY

CITY OF ESCONDIDO

CITY ATTORNEY'S OFFICE
CIVIC CENTER PLAZA
201 North Broadway, Escondido
California 92025-2738
(619) 741-4608
FAX (619) 741-7541

August 13, 1992

Deanne Stone
Counsel, Legal Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Alperin Advice Letter No. I-91-324

Dear Mrs. Stone:

I have received the Savage Advice Letter, dated August 19, 1991 in which a
reference is made to the Alperin Advice Letter, No. I-91-324. May I please have
a copy of the Alperin Advice Letter.

Also in the August 19 letter it states, "The proposed regulation would create
standards and procedures for agencies to follow so that fee-paying project
applicant, such as a developer, would not be considered a 'source of income'. . . ."
Our office would like to know whether the proposed regulation was adopted.

A copy of the letters I have are attached for ease of reference.

Yours very truly,

Maria Escobedo
Executive Secretary

Attachments

NOTLCM

JERRY C. HARMON, MAYOR     CARLA DeDOMINICIS, MAYOR PRO TEM     KRIS MURPHY     SID HOLLINS     RICHARD A. FOSTER

Printed on Recycled Paper

embedding.qa_text: This is a letter from the City of Escondido, dated August 13, 1992, addressed to Deanne Stone, Counsel, Legal Division, California Fair Political Practices Commission. The letter requests a copy of the Alperin Advice Letter No. I-91-324, which was referenced in a previous Savage Advice Letter dated August 19, 1991. The letter also mentions that the August 19 letter states: "The proposed regulation would create standards and procedures for agencies to follow so that fee-paying project applicant, such as a developer, would not be considered a 'source of income'...". The writer expresses interest in whether the proposed regulation was adopted. The letter concludes with the signature of Maria Escobedo, Executive Secretary, and includes a note about attached documents and a reference to NOTE.CM.
